title: Observed](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/playground-series-s6e5/discussion/696380%22]},{%22title%22:%22Observed) stop-debt pressure from PitStop counts
summary: Track how many pit stops have already been observed and whether the car still appears to owe a strategic stop late in the race.
rationale: Chronological PitStop-derived rule-state features were among the stronger local families but were not submitted as often, making them promising underexplored candidates. They connect naturally to mandatory stop requirements and late-race pressure.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race sequence, compute prior observed stop count, stop count including the current row, remaining assumed stop debt before and after the current row, stop debt per remaining lap, and finish-capable-but-still-owes-stop interactions. Test the block with and without current PitStop included.
expected_effect: Small to medium AUC gain with medium confidence.
risk: Counting future pit stops would directly leak. Current-row PitStop is allowed, but a prior-only ablation should be reported.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
